title: [Feature request] Restore Zellij environment after system restart
body:
Currently, all the Zellij sessions are lost after the System restarts, it will be nice to have them restored for a seamless experience.

### Reference

- [tmux-resurrect](https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect) is a plugin for tmux used for this exact purpose.
  - https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect#about - lists the things that tmux-resurrect can restore
  - https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect/blob/master/docs/restoring_programs.md - has information on the programs that can be restored
- From discord, rough pointers on implementation
```
1. Persist the buffers of each pane to the hard-drive
2. Keep track of the running process in each pane and be able to restart it on startup
3. Somehow keep track of the actual application state of each pane 
(eg. if you're in vim and you are scrolled down to a specific place, you need to start there)
```

title: Allow automatic light/dark mode detection from applications running within zellij (CSI 2031)
body:
Several terminal emulators now allow for automatic switching between light and dark themes, and allow applications running within the terminal to subscribe to theme changes. Automatic switching off all applications together is important to prevent situations where bright white and black windows end up side by side on a screen.

It would be great if zellij would understand CSI 2031, so that it could proxy these requests from applications to the outer terminal.

A few other terminal emulators and applications have implemented a simple protocol for dark/light mode detection and update notifications. For documentation, see: https://github.com/contour-terminal/contour/blob/f3c3334aa5c861348c5bbe8ffe572c872eef2e08/docs/vt-extensions/color-palette-update-notifications.md

Terminal emulators that are implementing CSI 2031 are:
- Ghostty
- Contour
- Kitty

Applications that take advantage of CSI 2031 are:
- Neovim: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/31350
- (probably others.)

The request here is to make sure that applications running in zellij can query the current theme (light/dark), but can also use CSI 2031 to subscribe to theme updates.

One edge case that would complicate things for zellij is a situation where a single window is attached in two different terminals, one with a light theme and one with a dark theme. In that case zellij would have to choose what to present to the applications.

Related issues on other projects:
- tmux: https://github.com/tmux/tmux/issues/4269
- wezterm: https://github.com/wez/wezterm/issues/6454

title: Feature: mouse support
body:
We should allow switching pane focus as well as resizing panes with the mouse.
We should also allow copying text from within a pane (and not including the text from the surrounding panes) when marking it with a mouse.

Other mouse suggestions are of course very welcome :)

title: Sixel support
body:
Several terminals already support [Sixels](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sixel), like [xterm](https://invisible-island.net/xterm/) (in vt340 mode), [wezterm](https://github.com/wez/wezterm), [mlterm](http://mlterm.sourceforge.net/), [foot](https://codeberg.org/dnkl/foot), ([kitty](https://sw.kovidgoyal.net/kitty/graphics-protocol.html) has its own thing) while others are working on it like [Alacritty](https://github.com/alacritty/alacritty/issues/910) and [Microsoft Terminal](https://github.com/microsoft/Terminal/issues/448).

The thing is right now there's no yet a terminal multiplexer that really supports sixels. Screen and tmux don't and probably never will. (Althought there's been a series of tmux forks trying to fix that ([latest](https://github.com/csdvrx/sixel-tmux/tree/master)) they're unofficial, unstable, and personally I've never been able to make them work).

It would be beyond fantastic if Zellij would be the first one to support this feature, and help bridge the gap in the road into a future where CLI apps can display pixel graphics inside terminal emulators. Soon [more interesting apps will be created](https://github.com/search?q=sixel) that makes use of it.


Disclaimer: I'm currently developing the Rust bindings for [notcurses](https://github.com/dankamongmen/notcurses) which will feature pixel support in the next [version 2.3.0](https://github.com/dankamongmen/notcurses/milestone/20).

title: Feature: rename an existing session
body:
First of all, thanks a lot for this great project!
Currently it's only possible to set the name when creating the session. The possibility to rename an existing session would be great though.

title: Feature: Implement persistent sessions
body: Mosaic should be able to run in the background regardless of whether a terminal window is open or not. New terminal windows should be able to 'attach' to existing sessions or create new ones.

title: Reloading the config at runtime
body:
This Feature organizes tasks for the end goal of allowing users to modify and reload zellij configuration files at runtime.

The following features are subsets of this feature and must be included in implementation:

- #1405 
- #2249 
- #2645 

This also requires distinguishing whether each option allows changes at runtime. For example, It is difficult to apply ​​to options such as `default_mode` or `mirror_session` when reloading. Instead, I think we can handle like UI related options such as `simplified_ui` or `pane_frames`

title: Configure keybindings at runtime
body:
In addition to allowing keybindings to be configured through the configuration file, we should create a plugin that allows their configuration at runtime for the running session, as well as exporting said configuration to a configuration file for persistence across sessions.

Depends on: #771

title: Feature request: Ability to rename a session
body:
I am really not a fan of the auto-generated session names! They are weird and difficult to remember!

I would like #1907 to be implemented, but I would also like a way to rename the session after it has been started, as I do not always know what I am going to use the session for.

title: Unable to send ctrl-g without rebinding in the config (one liner)
body:
## Description

With the default config I can't seem to find a way to send `Ctrl-g` to any programs I run in zellij because it's the key to both enter and exist the locked mode.

You can send any other key that's already used by the default config by entering the locked mode with `Ctrl-g`, then hitting the key you want to send, and leaving locked mode with `Ctrl-g` again, but that doesn't work for `Ctrl-g` itself.

## Information

- zellij 0.6.0
- Arch Linux 5.11.16-zen1-1-zen

title: feature / ability to remove "Zellij (session-name)" from top bar
body:
When using work path as session name (see: https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/issues/2171 ) the top bar becomes cluttered.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/82210878/219704400-68b1d5f4-261a-486a-99b8-f2f32664a333.png)

I would prefer to not have it at all and only show the tabs.

Like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/82210878/219705861-f687873a-5175-4932-a620-7b340a842faa.png)


Thanks!

title: Implement functionality propagate (sync) all panes either type or paste text
body:
I would like to ask you to implement functionality propagate to all panes to type or paste text to all active
windows. It is usefull, when you have open multiple ssh session to systems and you want to execute same
command on all of them. It's similar as is implemented in tmux. 
[Here is example with video](https://blog.sanctum.geek.nz/sync-tmux-panes/)
Thank you.

title: forward osc 52 copy events
body: Handle osc 52 copy that is sent from applications running inside zellij, to allow for example a nested `zellij` instance to set clipboard contents.

title: Feature request: mouse any-event Tracking (1003)
body:
Reference: https://www.invisible-island.net/xterm/ctlseqs/ctlseqs.html#h3-Any-event-tracking

This would round out mouse support, permitting 1) applications to know the mouse position always, and 2) implement focus-follows-mouse for panes.

title: Support various `Action` commands for server-client communication
body: ### Overview `Tmux` supports a wide range of Action Commands. And this can be classified into three main parts. * Clients and Sessions * Windows(Tabs) and Panes * Key bindings Each category has the following detailed command or purpose. (The comparison was focused on the purpose rather than the details.) #### Clients and Sessions | Purpose | Tmux | Zellij | Status | |:----------------------:|:----------------:|:--------------------------------------:|:-----------:| | attach target session | `attach-session` | `attach` | Done | | list all sessions | `list-sessions` | `list-sessions` | Done | | destroy target session | `kill-session` | `kill-session` and `kill-all-sessions` | Done | | rename target session | `rename-session` | #998 | Done | | lock target session | `lock-session` | #1265 | Done | | detach target client | `detach-client` | #1265 | Done | | switch target client | `switch-client` | None | #884[1](#footnote_1) | There is also a `start-server` command in `tmux`, but in `zellij`, it can be executed through `--server` options. and it is also related to #1062 #### Key bindings | Purpose | Tmux | Zellij | Status | |:-----------------------------------:|:-----------:|:------:|:------:| | send a key or keys to a window(tab) | `send-keys` | Done | #1265 | | send a text or buffers to a window(tab) | `send-keys`[2](#footnote_2) | Done | #809, #1265 | tmux also has `send-prefix`, but this doesn't seem necessary for `zellij`. If there is anything to be added or not necessary, please let me know 😃 **EDIT: Most of the features are currently implemented, but I'll keep this issue for issue tracking.** --- 1. It seems to be a partially implementable issue for switch-client. 2. In the case of `tmux`, one command covers both `key` and `text`.

title: [Feature Request] Send text/buffer to pane
body:
# Feature Request Description

Sends text directly to another pane on an existing session. As simple as that.
Example command:  `zellij send-text --session <name> --pane <index> <text or command that shows text like 'cat'>`

## Use cases

1. Editor plugins (e.g. vim-slime for neovim and vim)
2. Saving a finite list of buffers so it can be used later.
3. REPL Workflow based on point (1). For users of Julia, Python, R, and other languages that has a REPL.
4. Save a mouse selection to the list of buffers (?)

An example for points 1 and 3 in tmux with vim-slime plugin for neovim:


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/66054069/139166920-87ed9774-e336-4455-af3d-8aaddf052bf0.mp4

### Todo

- [ ] 1. Implement the cli command somewhere in https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/blob/main/zellij-utils/src/cli.rs#L51 (in progress)
- [ ] 2. Add the handler somewhere in https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/blob/main/src/main.rs#L85 (idle)
- [ ] 3. Create a new instruction for the command (passive)
- [ ] 4. Set the environment variables in each session/pane/tab to facilitate doing this when you're inside Zellij (last to do)

title: Add default color themes
body:
Add default color themes.

Possibly even support for [base16](https://github.com/chriskempson/base16).

title: Feature request: zellij attach --create
body:
A command that automatically creates (if it doesn't exist) and joins the given named-session would be useful.

It can come in handy in creating quick project-specific sessions, for instance:

```
zellij attach --create $(basename `pwd`)
```
